title: Plumb structured deferral reasons through scheduler core and reporter contract
description: Add a bead_deferred(bead, reason) hook to the scheduler reporter contract and update scheduler deferral paths to emit non-empty human-readable reasons for dependency, worktree, and file-conflict skips.
acceptance_criteria: SchedulerReporter defines bead_deferred(bead, reason) with the new contract.
Every scheduler deferral path reports a non-empty reason string through the reporter.
Deferral reason strings distinguish at least dependency, worktree, and file-conflict cases.
Core deferral reporting integrates cleanly with the updated scheduler execution flow.

handoff_summary.completed: 1. reporter.py: renamed bead_deferred parameter from summary to reason. 2. core.py: added BEAD_DONE import; improved _find_conflict_reason to distinguish worktree (same-feature-tree/no-scope) vs file-scope conflicts with specific reason strings; added _report_dependency_deferrals() called from _select_beads_for_dispatch when a reporter is present, emitting bead_deferred for READY beads whose deps are not done; added _dep_is_done() helper.
handoff_summary.risks: Renaming the protocol parameter from summary to reason is source-compatible for all positional callers (confirmed no keyword callers). Existing bead_deferred implementations in CliSchedulerReporter and TuiSchedulerReporter still use summary as their local parameter name — that is fine for Protocol conformance but a follow-up tester or reviewer may want consistency. The new _report_dependency_deferrals loop calls storage.list_beads() on every fill iteration; for large bead graphs this is O(n) but is already the pattern used by _reevaluate_blocked. Dependency deferrals do NOT increment result.deferred (file-conflict count preserved).
handoff_summary.next_action: Run tester bead to verify all scheduler tests pass with the renamed protocol parameter and new deferral reporting paths.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: CliSchedulerReporter (cli/commands/run.py:50) and TuiSchedulerReporter (tui/app.py:1075) both implement bead_deferred with a local parameter named summary — not reason. Protocol conformance is structural (duck typing), so this works at runtime, but sibling beads touching those files should be aware of the local naming mismatch if they refactor those methods.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Dependency deferrals are reported only through the reporter (not added to result.deferred) to avoid inflating the file-conflict deferred count in the cycle summary JSON. Rationale: result.deferred currently means "beads skipped due to file-scope conflict this cycle"; mixing in dependency-blocked beads would change the semantics of a field that the CLI already surfaces to users. Reporter-only events are sufficient for observability. The _report_dependency_deferrals loop is called only when a reporter is present (guarded by if reporter:) so there is zero overhead in the common headless path. _dep_is_done wraps storage.load_bead in a try/except to match the resilience pattern already used by storage.dependency_satisfied for corrupt dep references. The _find_conflict_reason rewrite reuses _beads_conflict for the boolean gate, then independently computes same_feature_tree for the reason string — this avoids duplicating the conflict logic while keeping the reason path readable.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) bead_deferred is called with a reason containing "dependency not done" when a READY bead has an unfinished dep; (2) bead_deferred is NOT called twice for the same dep-blocked bead in a single run_once() cycle (deferred_this_cycle gate); (3) _find_conflict_reason returns "worktree in use by in-progress X" for same-feature-tree/no-scope conflict; (4) _find_conflict_reason returns "file-scope conflict with in-progress X" for scope-overlap conflict; (5) existing file-conflict deferral path still writes to result.deferred and calls storage.update_bead; (6) dep-blocked beads are NOT added to result.deferred. Regression: all existing scheduler core and execution tests should still pass.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: CliSchedulerReporter and TuiSchedulerReporter still use summary as the local parameter name in their bead_deferred implementations. This is harmless for runtime but inconsistent with the updated Protocol contract. Cleaning up those implementations is outside the scope of this bead (expected_files excludes run.py and app.py). The _report_dependency_deferrals loop calls storage.list_beads() which may load all beads from disk on every fill iteration — acceptable for current bead graph sizes but worth noting for future scaling.

conflict_risks: CliSchedulerReporter (cli/commands/run.py:50) and TuiSchedulerReporter (tui/app.py:1075) both implement bead_deferred with a local parameter named summary — not reason. Protocol conformance is structural (duck typing), so this works at runtime, but sibling beads touching those files should be aware of the local naming mismatch if they refactor those methods.

execution_history.summary: Added structured deferral reason reporting to scheduler core and reporter contract. The reporter's bead_deferred parameter was renamed from summary to reason. Two new deferral categories now emit through the reporter: (1) dependency deferrals — READY beads with unsatisfied dependencies now fire bead_deferred with "dependency not done: <id>, ..." instead of being silently filtered; (2) worktree conflicts — same-feature-tree no-scope conflicts now emit "worktree in use by in-progress <id> (no file scope defined)" instead of the generic file-scope message. File-scope conflicts now emit "file-scope conflict with in-progress <id>".

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `{{LANGUAGE}}` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `{{BUILD_CHECK_COMMAND}}`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `{{TEST_COMMAND}}` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
